question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign indicates a traffic control person ahead. Traffic control persons (also known as flag persons) may direct traffic at construction (work) zones by using hand signals and STOP/SLOW paddles. Slow down and follow all directions of the traffic control person.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: This lane exits to the right.
There is a slight bend or curve in the road ahead.
Keep right if there is faster traffic.
There is a sharp bend or curve in the road ahead.

chosenAnswer: This lane exits to the right.
correctAnswer: There is a slight bend or curve in the road ahead.
explanation: This sign warns of a slight bend or curve in the road ahead.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Bumpy or Uneven Pavement Ahead” road sign. Slow down.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You must be driving a school bus to enter this area.
School buses load or unload in this area without using the flashing red lights or the stop arm.
School buses load or unload in this area by turning on the flashing red lights and extending the stop arm.

chosenAnswer: You must be driving a school bus to enter this area.
correctAnswer: School buses load or unload in this area without using the flashing red lights or the stop arm.
explanation: This sign marks a loading zone for school buses. In this area, school buses load or unload passengers without using the flashing red lights or the stop arm.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Slight bend or curve in the road ahead

explanation: This sign means that there is a closed lane ahead. If you're in that lane, adjust your speed to merge with traffic in the lane indicated by the arrow.

question: What does this construction sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Slow down and obey the flagger's directions.
The pavement is milled or grooved.
There is road work operation ahead.

chosenAnswer: Slow down and obey the flagger's directions.
correctAnswer: There is road work operation ahead.
explanation: This is a “Road Work Ahead” sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You may park in the designated area during the posted times.
You may not park in the designated area during the posted times.

chosenAnswer: You may park in the designated area during the posted times.
correctAnswer: You may park in the designated area during the posted times.
explanation: A green circle indicates permission. This regulatory sign means that you may park in the designated area for two hours during the posted times.

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign indicates that the road ahead is divided by a median. Keep right of it.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign is a "School Crossing Ahead" road sign. Follow the directions of the crossing guard or school safety patroller.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Steep Hill Ahead” road sign. This sign says that you are about to enter a steep 12% descent. Check your brakes. To reduce wear on your brakes, shift into a lower gear even if your vehicle has an automatic transmission.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a sign indicating that a railway crossing is ahead. Slow down, watch for trains and be prepared to stop if a train is approaching.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: No bicycles allowed on this road

correctAnswer: No bicycles allowed on this road
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This regulatory sign means that bicycles are not allowed.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: This lane is for bicycles only.
This road is an official bicycle route.
There is a bicycle crossing ahead.
No pedestrians are allowed on this road.

chosenAnswer: This lane is for bicycles only.
correctAnswer: This road is an official bicycle route.
explanation: This road is an official bicycle route. Watch for cyclists and be prepared to share the road with them.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you may proceed if you're on the opposite side of the road.
When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must stop at least 20 metres from the bus.
When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must stop at any point before reaching the bus.
When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must slow down to 25 km/h and proceed with caution.

chosenAnswer: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you may proceed if you're on the opposite side of the road.
correctAnswer: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must stop at least 20 metres from the bus.
explanation: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm extended, you must stop at least 20 metres from the bus, regardless of which side of the road the bus is on. Remain stopped until the stop sign arm is retracted and the red lights stop flashing. (The only exception is if the bus is stopped on the opposite side of a divided highway. In that case, you don't have to stop for it.)

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: This emblem on the rear of the vehicle identifies it as a slow-moving vehicle (one whose speed is 40 km/h or less). Slow-moving vehicles include farm equipment and some construction equipment. If you see this emblem on a vehicle you are following, slow down and be patient.

question: A sign with a green circle

answerOptions.text: indicates a route for large trucks.

explanation: A regulatory sign with a green circle is a permissive sign. It means you may or must do the activity shown inside the circle.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Bicycle Crossing Ahead” road sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Maximum safe speed on the ramp
Maximum safe speed for the curve
Maximum speed limit on the highway

chosenAnswer: Maximum safe speed on the ramp
correctAnswer: Maximum speed limit on the highway
explanation: This is a “Maximum Speed Limit on the Highway” road sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Deer Crossing” road sign. Watch for deer and be prepared to stop for them.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Watch for Falling Rocks” road sign. Drive with extra caution and be prepared to avoid a collision.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Shows distances to towns or cities
Shows directions to nearby towns or cities
Shows the exits on the upcoming roundabout and where they will take you

chosenAnswer: Shows distances to towns or cities
correctAnswer: Shows the exits on the upcoming roundabout and where they will take you
explanation: This sign shows the exits on the upcoming roundabout and where they will take you. After you pass this sign and approach the roundabout, you should also encounter a roundabout warning sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: No stopping for more than 3 minutes
No smoking for more than 3 minutes
Idling permitted for at least 3 minutes
No idling for more than 3 minutes

chosenAnswer: No stopping for more than 3 minutes
correctAnswer: No idling for more than 3 minutes
explanation: This type of sign is a “No Idling” sign. This particular sign means that you may not idle your engine here for more than 3 minutes.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This regulatory sign means that cars and other vehicles should not pass.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: A traffic control person is ahead. Drive slowly and watch for instructions.
Watch for pedestrians and be prepared to share the road with them.
A survey crew is working on the road ahead.

correctAnswer: A survey crew is working on the road ahead.
explanation: This sign is seen at construction zones where survey crews may be working. Proceed with caution.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a pedestrian crossover. Be prepared to stop and yield the right-of-way to pedestrians. You may not pass within 30 metres of a pedestrian crossover.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: The speed limit is 40 km/h at all times.
During school hours when the yellow lights are flashing, the speed limit is 40 km/h.

chosenAnswer: The speed limit is 40 km/h at all times.
correctAnswer: During school hours when the yellow lights are flashing, the speed limit is 40 km/h.
explanation: During school hours when the yellow lights are flashing, you must always follow the speed limit shown. This sign means that the speed limit when the yellow lights are flashing is 40 km/h.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Danger; there is a sharp turn ahead.
There is a playground to the right.

correctAnswer: Danger; there is a sharp turn ahead.
explanation: This sign warns of a sudden turn in the road ahead in the direction shown by the arrow. Slow down and proceed with care.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This regulatory sign indicates that left turns are prohibited here.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This regulatory sign indicates that U-turns are prohibited here.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a "Do Not Enter" sign. It indicates that you cannot enter the roadway at which the sign is posted. You will see such signs at roadway openings you are not to enter or where you would be driving the wrong way on a one-way roadway. You will see them at exit ramps, in crossovers on divided roadways, and at numerous locations on one-way roads.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: This is the route to an airport.
This is a landing area for helicopters.

correctAnswer: This is the route to an airport.
explanation: Information signs are square signs with white symbols. This information sign indicates that an airport is ahead.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: There is a hidden intersection ahead.

explanation: This is a Merging Traffic sign. Two roads going in the same direction are about to join into one. Drivers on both roads are equally responsible for ensuring that traffic merges smoothly and safely.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Keep right of the traffic island.
A road is branching off ahead.
There is a passing lane ahead.
There is a slight bend or curve in the road ahead.

chosenAnswer: Keep right of the traffic island.
correctAnswer: There is a passing lane ahead.
explanation: This regulatory sign means you must stay in the right lane except when passing on two-lane sections where climbing or passing lanes are provided.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This sign means that pedestrians are not allowed to use this section of the road.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Stop Sign Ahead” road sign. Be prepared to stop at the stop sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign indicates a movable bridge ahead. The bridge lifts or swings open to allow boats to pass. Slow down and prepare to stop in case the bridge is open.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a yield sign. When you approach a yield sign, slow down and prepare to yield to pedestrians and approaching traffic. You may also have to stop for them, so be ready.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: Information signs are square signs with white symbols. This information sign indicates a hospital nearby.

question: This road sign means that parking is permitted

answerOptions.text: only for vehicles displaying a valid Accessible Parking Permit.
for pregnant women and mothers with young children.

chosenAnswer: only for vehicles displaying a valid Accessible Parking Permit.
correctAnswer: only for vehicles displaying a valid Accessible Parking Permit.
explanation: This sign is placed in the areas where parking is permitted only for vehicles displaying a valid Accessible Parking Permit.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a direction sign, showing the directions to various cities and towns.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Maximum safe speed for a curve
Maximum safe speed on the shoulder
Recommended speed in a construction zone

correctAnswer: Maximum safe speed for a curve
explanation: Posted under a curve warning, this sign shows the maximum safe speed for the curve.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Snowmobiles may not use this road.
There is a snowmobile repair shop ahead.

explanation: A green circle indicates permission. This regulatory sign means that snowmobiles may use this road.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You must not drive in the right-hand lane under any circumstances.
You must exit if you remain in the right-hand lane.
You may exit if you remain in the right-hand lane.
This is the end of the highway; you must move into the right-hand lane.

chosenAnswer: You must not drive in the right-hand lane under any circumstances.
correctAnswer: You must exit if you remain in the right-hand lane.
explanation: This sign means that the right-hand lane leads to an exit ramp off this roadway. If you don't want to exit this roadway, move out of the right-hand lane.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign means that there is a hidden school bus stop ahead. School buses stop with flashing red lights there. Slow down, drive with extra caution, and watch for children or a school bus with flashing red lights.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You may not turn right on a red light.
You may not turn left on a red light.
There is no road on the right side of the intersection.
You may not turn right on a green light.

chosenAnswer: You may not turn right on a red light.
correctAnswer: You may not turn right on a red light.
explanation: This is a "No Right Turn on Red" sign. Where this sign is posted, you may not turn right on a red light unless there is also a green arrow light pointing right.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Two or more occupants must be in the vehicle to use this lane.
This is a highway with two express lanes.
Two lanes will merge into one.

chosenAnswer: Two or more occupants must be in the vehicle to use this lane.
correctAnswer: Two or more occupants must be in the vehicle to use this lane.
explanation: This type of sign indicates that the lane is a High-Occupancy Vehicle (HOV) lane. This particular sign indicates that only buses and vehicles with two or more occupants (i.e., one or more passengers) may use this lane. However, there are a couple of exceptions: Plug-in hybrid electric vehicles and full-battery electric vehicles with the "Green" licence plate may use this lane regardless of the number of occupants. Motorcyclists may also use this lane even if they are not carrying passengers.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign indicates an underpass ahead with only 3.9 metres of vertical clearance. (In Ontario, trucks can legally be up to 4.15 metres tall.)

question: This driver is using a hand signal. The driver intends to
explanation: This driver intends to turn left.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This type of warning sign gives advance notice of a reduction in the number of lanes ahead. This particular sign indicates that the right lane ends ahead. All traffic in the right lane must merge left.

question: A broken yellow line on your side of the roadway means that you may

answerOptions.text: pass if the way is clear.
pass only under ideal driving conditions.

chosenAnswer: pass if the way is clear.
correctAnswer: pass if the way is clear.
explanation: A broken yellow line on your side of the roadway means that you may pass if the way is clear. A solid yellow line on your side means that you are not allowed to pass.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: The pavement is slippery when wet.
The pavement has been milled or grooved.

chosenAnswer: The pavement is slippery when wet.
correctAnswer: The pavement is slippery when wet.
explanation: This warning sign means that the road is slippery when wet. In wet weather, slow down and proceed with extra caution.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Slow down and yield to approaching vehicles.
Slow down and proceed with caution.
There is a stop sign ahead.
Stop and yield to approaching vehicles.

chosenAnswer: Slow down and yield to approaching vehicles.
correctAnswer: Stop and yield to approaching vehicles.
explanation: This is a stop sign. You must come to a complete stop and yield the right-of-way to approaching vehicles before you proceed.

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: All railway crossings on public roads in Ontario are marked with large red-and-white crossbuck (“X”) signs. When a train is approaching, you must stop and yield to the train. Remember: The train cannot yield to you.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Speed limit changes to 50 km/h ahead
Maximum speed of 50 km/h from this sign to the next one
Speed limit for rural school zones

chosenAnswer: Speed limit changes to 50 km/h ahead
correctAnswer: Speed limit changes to 50 km/h ahead
explanation: This regulatory sign indicates that there is a speed limit change ahead. You must be prepared to slow down to the posted speed when you see another speed limit sign without an arrow.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: This road turns sharply right ahead.
Traffic must exit to the right.
This road turns slightly right ahead.

chosenAnswer: This road turns sharply right ahead.
correctAnswer: This road turns sharply right ahead.
explanation: This sign warns of a sharp bend or turn to the right in the road ahead. Slow down before you get to it.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Keep to the right of the traffic island.
There is a sharp bend or turn in the road ahead.
Traffic must travel in one direction only.
Vehicles in this lane must turn right.

chosenAnswer: Keep to the right of the traffic island.
correctAnswer: Traffic must travel in one direction only.
explanation: This is a One Way sign. On this street, traffic may travel only in the direction of the arrow.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You may exit if you remain in the right-hand lane.
You must not drive in the right-hand lane.
This is a two-way left-turn lane.
The highway ends ahead; you must move into the right-hand lane.

chosenAnswer: You may exit if you remain in the right-hand lane.
correctAnswer: This is a two-way left-turn lane.
explanation: This sign indicates a two-way left-turn lane. This is a lane in the centre of the roadway that can be used by vehicles travelling in either direction to make left turns.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You may not park between here and the corner.

explanation: This is a “Right Turn Only” sign. You must turn right from this lane.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Road ahead turns left, then right
Road ahead turns right, then left

chosenAnswer: Road ahead turns left, then right
correctAnswer: Road ahead turns right, then left
explanation: This sign indicates a double curve ahead. The road ahead first turns right, then left. (If the road ahead had three or more curves, a Winding Road sign would be posted instead.)

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: There is a winding road ahead.
Pass other traffic on the right.
Keep right of the obstacle ahead.
The road turns right and then left.

chosenAnswer: There is a winding road ahead.
correctAnswer: Keep right of the obstacle ahead.
explanation: This sign indicates a divider, traffic island or other obstacle ahead. Keep right of it.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Truck entrance ahead on the right

correctAnswer: Truck entrance ahead on the right
explanation: This sign means there is a truck entrance ahead on the right.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: It is safe to enter this area.
Be more aware of pedestrians and your speed in this area.

chosenAnswer: It is safe to enter this area.
correctAnswer: Be more aware of pedestrians and your speed in this area.
explanation: This sign indicates a community safety zone. This is an area where according to the community, speeding or aggressive driving poses a special risk to pedestrians. Traffic offences committed within this zone are subject to increased fines. Obey all speed limits and drive carefully.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: The "Do Not Block Intersection" sign warns you not to enter the intersection if traffic is blocked beyond it, because you might end up blocking the intersection for cross traffic. This warning sign is rectangular rather than diamond shaped because of its relatively long text.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign indicates that the divided highway ends. Keep right and watch for oncoming vehicles.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: There may be water flowing over the road.
There is a movable bridge ahead.
There is a construction zone ahead.

chosenAnswer: There may be water flowing over the road.
correctAnswer: There may be water flowing over the road.
explanation: This sign indicates that water may flow over this road.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Do not enter during the times and days posted.
Buses are not allowed in this lane during the times and days posted.
No stopping is allowed during the times and days posted.
Only certain types of vehicles are allowed in this lane during the times and days posted.

chosenAnswer: Do not enter during the times and days posted.
correctAnswer: Only certain types of vehicles are allowed in this lane during the times and days posted.
explanation: This is a “High-Occupancy Vehicle (HOV)" sign. During certain times, an HOV lane is open only to such high-occupancy vehicles as buses, taxis, and passenger vehicles with a specified minimum number of occupants. This HOV sign indicates that only passenger vehicles with at least 3 occupants (i.e., at least 2 passengers) may use this lane.

question: This symbol indicates
explanation: A red circle with a slash through it means the activity shown inside the ring is not allowed.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Vehicles cannot change lanes into or out of a high-occupancy vehicle (HOV) lane in this area.
There is a railway crossing ahead. Be alert for trains.
There is a hazard close to the edge of the road. The downward lines show the side on which you may safely pass.
This is a pedestrian crossover. Be prepared to stop and yield the right-of-way to pedestrians.

chosenAnswer: Vehicles cannot change lanes into or out of a high-occupancy vehicle (HOV) lane in this area.
correctAnswer: Vehicles cannot change lanes into or out of a high-occupancy vehicle (HOV) lane in this area.
explanation: This sign means that vehicles cannot change lanes into or out of a high-occupancy (HOV) lane in this area.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign indicates the end of the paved surface.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Drive to the left of the sign.
Drive to the right of the sign.
You may stop and rest here.

chosenAnswer: Drive to the left of the sign.
correctAnswer: Drive to the right of the sign.
explanation: This is a hazard warning sign. The downward lines indicate the side on which you may bypass the hazard (in this case, to its right).

question: What does a pair of these signs mean?

answerOptions.text: Do not stop in the area between the signs except to load or unload passengers or merchandise.
Do not stop in the area between the signs.
Do not park in the area between the signs.
Do not stand in the area between the signs.

chosenAnswer: Do not stop in the area between the signs except to load or unload passengers or merchandise.
correctAnswer: Do not stop in the area between the signs.
explanation: This is a No Stopping sign. It means that you are not allowed to stop here, even for a moment. These signs are typically used in pairs or groups.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Follow these signs until you return to your regular route.
This lane is closed ahead; merge into another lane.
There is construction work one kilometre ahead.

correctAnswer: Follow these signs until you return to your regular route.
explanation: This is a temporary condition sign. These signs warn of unusual temporary conditions such as road work zones, diversions, detours, lane closures, or traffic control people on the road. This particular sign is a detour marker. Keep following these detour markers until they return you to your regular route.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Bus entrance ahead on the right
Truck entrance ahead on the right

chosenAnswer: Bus entrance ahead on the right
correctAnswer: Bus entrance ahead on the right
explanation: This sign indicates an upcoming bus entrance on the right. Vehicles should be prepared to yield to buses entering the roadway.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: There is a sharp curve to the right.
Stay to the right of the centre island.

chosenAnswer: There is a sharp curve to the right.
correctAnswer: There is a sharp curve to the right.
explanation: Chevron (arrowhead) signs are posted in groups to guide drivers around sharp curves in the road.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You may stop temporarily to load or unload passengers but not merchandise.
You may stop temporarily to load or unload passengers or merchandise.
You may stop temporarily to load or unload merchandise but not passengers.
You may not stop for any reason.

chosenAnswer: You may stop temporarily to load or unload passengers but not merchandise.
correctAnswer: You may stop temporarily to load or unload passengers but not merchandise.
explanation: According to the Ontario Highway Traffic Act, "No Parking" means you may stop only temporarily to load or unload passengers or merchandise. "No Standing" means you may stop only temporarily to load or unload passengers but not merchandise. ("No Standing" signs are often posted at bus stops and taxi stands.) "No Stopping" means you may not stop for any reason, not even temporarily, unless you're directed to do so by a traffic sign or signal or a police officer.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: There is someone directing traffic ahead.
There is a stop sign ahead.
There are no traffic signals on this road.
There is a traffic signal ahead.

chosenAnswer: There is someone directing traffic ahead.
correctAnswer: There is a traffic signal ahead.
explanation: This warning sign indicates that there is a traffic signal ahead. Be prepared to stop.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: The road is slippery when wet.
Share the road with oncoming traffic.

chosenAnswer: The road is slippery when wet.
correctAnswer: Share the road with oncoming traffic.
explanation: This sign means you have to share the road with oncoming traffic.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Truck entrance ahead on the right
Fire truck entrance ahead on the right
Bus entrance ahead on the right

chosenAnswer: Truck entrance ahead on the right
correctAnswer: Fire truck entrance ahead on the right
explanation: This sign indicates an upcoming fire truck entrance on the right. Prepare to yield to fire trucks entering the roadway.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Road Branching Off Ahead” road sign. This sign indicates a side road branching off to the right.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign is a “Winding Road Ahead” sign. A "winding road" is a road with at least three curves. Slow down and keep right.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This regulatory sign indicates that going straight is prohibited here.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign indicates that there is a narrow bridge ahead. The width of the road will be less than the part of the road you are currently driving on. Slow down at the narrow passage.

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: This sign features the international symbol of access for persons with disabilities. The sign indicates that a person in a wheelchair may use a handicapped parking space.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You must stop for a school bus if your turn signals are flashing.
You may pass a school bus if its red signals are flashing.
You must stop for a school bus if the traffic lights are flashing.
You must stop for a school bus if its red signals are flashing.

chosenAnswer: You must stop for a school bus if your turn signals are flashing.
correctAnswer: You must stop for a school bus if its red signals are flashing.
explanation: This sign means that you must stop for a school bus when its red signals are flashing.

question: This road sign indicates

answerOptions.text: the direction of travel for each lane.
you must not enter this area.
you may drive in any direction from any lane.

chosenAnswer: the direction of travel for each lane.
correctAnswer: the direction of travel for each lane.
explanation: These signs, above the road or as pavement markings, tell drivers in each lane the directions in which they must travel. This particular sign means that drivers in the left lane must turn left, drivers in the centre lane must either turn right or go straight, and drivers in the right lane must turn right.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign warns that there is an intersection ahead. The arrow shows indicates which road has the right-of-way.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “School Zone” sign. A school is nearby. Proceed with caution because there may be schoolchildren around.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: A highway interchange is 300 metres ahead.

chosenAnswer: A highway interchange is 300 metres ahead.
explanation: This sign indicates that a roundabout is 300 metres ahead. A roundabout is a circular intersection in which traffic circulates counterclockwise around a central island. Slow down and prepare to yield to the traffic already in the roundabout.

question: This driver is using a hand signal. The driver intends to
explanation: This hand signal indicates that the driver intends to slow down or stop. This hand signal is the equivalent of the brake lights on the driver's vehicle.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Slow down when you are in the right lane.
The right lane is for faster traffic.

explanation: This sign is usually posted on multilane roadways. It means just what it says: slower traffic should stay in the rightmost lane except when turning left or avoiding an obstruction or other hazard.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Do not make a U-turn except during the times shown.
Do not turn left except during the times shown.
Do not turn left during the times shown.

chosenAnswer: Do not make a U-turn except during the times shown.
correctAnswer: Do not turn left during the times shown.
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This sign means that left turns are prohibited during the times shown on the sign (in this case, 7:00 a.m. – 9:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. – 6:00 p.m.).

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You must stop for a red light at this intersection.
There are police officers at this intersection.
Vehicles that stop at this red light will be photographed.
Vehicles that don't stop at this red light will be photographed.

chosenAnswer: You must stop for a red light at this intersection.
correctAnswer: Vehicles that don't stop at this red light will be photographed.
explanation: This intersection is monitored with a red light camera. The camera photographs vehicles that run a red light here. Their owners may be cited.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign indicates that the roadway narrows without a reduction in the number of lanes. There will be less lane width available. Slow down, stay in your lane and watch for oncoming vehicles.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: There is a concealed side road ahead.
There are no left turns allowed.
There are no right turns allowed.

chosenAnswer: There is a concealed side road ahead.
correctAnswer: There is a concealed side road ahead.
explanation: This warning sign indicates a hidden intersection ahead. There is a hidden side road ahead. And drivers on the side road may not be able to see you approaching. Slow down and proceed with caution.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign means that the pavement has been milled or grooved. Your vehicle's stopping ability may be affected, so obey the speed limit and drive with extra caution. Motorcyclists may experience reduced traction on these surfaces.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: There is a snowmobile repair shop ahead.

chosenAnswer: There is a snowmobile repair shop ahead.
explanation: This is a “Snowmobiles Cross This Road” road sign.

question: This driver is using a hand signal. The driver intends to
explanation: Hand signals can be used instead of your car's regular lighted turn and brake signals. This driver intends to turn right.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Side road near a railway ahead

explanation: This warning sign indicates a railway crossing ahead. You must slow down and proceed only if it is safe.

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: This sign means that there may be pedestrians ahead. Watch for pedestrians and be prepared to share the road with them.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: This lane is for buses only.
This lane is not for bicycles.
This lane is for trucks only.
This lane is for bicycles only.

chosenAnswer: This lane is for buses only.
correctAnswer: This lane is for bicycles only.
explanation: A diamond symbol indicates a reserved lane, a lane in which only certain kinds of vehicles may legally travel. This particular sign means that only bicycles may travel in this lane.
